GitHub Actions workflow for running pre-commit checks on pull requests and pushes to main and stable branches.

# Purpose
This GitHub Actions workflow file automates tasks for pull requests and pushes to the `main` and `stable` branches. It defines a job named `main` that runs on the `ubuntu-latest` environment. The workflow includes several steps: checking out the repository code, setting up the `uv` tool with caching enabled, configuring Python using a version specified in `pyproject.toml`, and caching pre-commit hooks. The workflow then executes pre-commit hooks using the `uv` tool, ensuring that any code changes adhere to predefined standards. Finally, it uses the `pre-commit-ci/lite-action` to perform additional checks, provided the workflow has not been cancelled.
